# cBrowse

cBrowse connects AI agents to the user's real signed-in browser through MCP.

## Endpoints

- Setup page: https://cbrowse.tech/
- MCP endpoint: https://cbrowse.tech/mcp
- Bridge WebSocket: wss://cbrowse.tech/bridge
- Raw cBrowse skill: https://cbrowse.tech/cbrowse-skill.md

## What It Does

- Pairs an installed browser extension with a browser-specific MCP endpoint.
- Lets agent clients reuse the browser's existing cookies, saved auth, tabs, and page state.
- Supports page inspection, navigation, clicking, typing, key presses, scrolling, and mouse movement.
- Helps agents extract data, analyze authenticated apps, and debug/test flows in the same browser context the user already has.
- Provides a raw skill file for clients that support extra behavioral instructions.

## Setup

1. Install the cBrowse extension.
2. Use the browser-specific MCP endpoint generated by that extension in your agent client.
3. If your client supports skills or extra agent instructions, use the raw cBrowse skill file.
4. Ask the agent to work inside the browser session that issued the route and claim tabs before destructive actions.

## Bridge Status

- Live bridge status is available at https://cbrowse.tech/api/status
- The landing page shows browser-link connectivity and the latest active agent/session information.
